t_ch: 多谢，年轻人。希望不会占用你太多时间。
t_en: Thank you, kid. I hope this won't take a lot of your time.

t_ch: 嚯嚯嚯…真神奇！想不到我这把老骨头还有看到它亮起来的一天啊。
t_en: Ohoho... Amazing! I didn't think I'd live to see this light up again...

t_ch: 这机器里留着我最珍贵的宝贝，能修好可真是太好了……
t_en: My most precious treasure is kept inside this machine. Thank goodness it's fixed...

t_ch: 谢谢，年轻人…谢谢。
t_en: Thank you, kid... Thank you.

t_ch: 不客气，你多保重。
t_en: You're welcome. Please take care.

t_ch: 嚯嚯…那么，你也多保重。
t_en: Hahaha... Take care of yourself as well.

t_ch: 你转身离开，又正欲回头再看一眼老人时，却发现那人早已杳无踪迹。
t_en: As you turn to leave, you try to get one last look at the old man, but he is already nowhere to be found.

t_ch: 哦？请留步，年轻人。你手上的是……
t_en: Oh? Wait, kid. You're holding...

t_ch: 哟，{F#姐们儿}{M#哥们儿}也是来下馆子的啊？
t_en: Yo! You're here for a feed too?

t_ch: 可惜他们今天还没开门——怎么，要不咱一起等着？跟你说啊，我可是这儿的常客了！他们家的「爆辣蓬蓬菇」可是一绝嘞……
t_en: Pity they haven't opened yet. Wanna wait for them together? I'm telling ya, I'm a regular here. Their Spicy Puffy Mushroom is an absolute treasure...

t_ch: 这家店已经废弃了。
t_en: This shop is abandoned.

t_ch: 啥？你可别唬我。这灯不还亮着吗？
t_en: Hah? Are you pulling my leg? Aren't the lights still on?

t_ch: 裂界的回响也能吃饭吗？
t_en: Can Fragmentum echoes eat?

t_ch: 什么裂界？回响又是啥？{F#姐们儿}{M#哥们儿}说的我咋都听不懂呢？
t_en: What's a Fragmentum? What's an echo? I ain't getting whatcha trying to say.

t_ch: 行了行了，咱们先等着吧。我看他们马上就开门咯——再不开门我可要饿坏了……
t_en: Whatever, let's just wait. I reckon they're about to open up. I'll be starving if they don't...

t_ch: （看来，不把饭端到他面前，他是不会罢休的……）
t_en: (Seems like he won't give up unless there's food before his face...)

t_ch: 怎么了，你想让我吃这个？这炒饭看着怪好吃的啊，你做的？
t_en: What, you want me to eat this? This fried rice looks pretty tasty. Did you make it?

t_ch: 行吧，反正我肚子也饿了——送到嘴边的肉哪有不吃的道理！
t_en: Alright, I'm hungry anyways — It ain't smart to pass up food shoved in front of my face!

t_ch: 这炒饭…你怎么调味的啊？咋一点味道都没有？吃进嘴里跟嚼矿渣似的……
t_en: This fried rice... What did you do with it? It has no flavor! It tastes like mine dust...

t_ch: 我就说你吃不了吧。
t_en: I told you that you can't eat.

t_ch: 意识到问题了吗？
t_en: Do you see the problem now?

t_ch: 难道是我舌头不灵了？咋回事，我不明白啊……
t_en: Did something happen to my tongue? What's going on? I don't get it...

t_ch: 你失去了物理意义上的身体。
t_en: You have lost the physical body forever.

t_ch: 你已经变成了一只幽灵…
t_en: You've turned into a ghost...

t_ch: 这么说…是我已经死了呗？
t_en: So... I'm dead?

t_ch: 那我为啥还能和你说话呢？难不成你也……
t_en: Then how come I can still talk to you? Are you also...

t_ch: 你只是裂界模拟诞生的产物。
t_en: You're just a creation emulated by the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 这话不太中听啊…
t_en: That's a bit rude...

t_ch: 哎，行吧。虽然我不太懂你说的那什么「裂界」到底是啥，但我好像已经理解了一切……
t_en: Yeah, alright. I don't really get what's this Fragmentum thing you talk about, but I think I understand...

t_ch: 这么看来…我岂不是吃不了好吃的了吗？那也太空虚了吧……
t_en: So... I can't eat tasty food anymore? That's really sad...

t_ch: 你看着面前的男人露出寂寞的神情，随后便如溃散的沙塔般于裂界的土地溶解。
t_en: The man before you shows a lonely expression before dissolving back into the Fragmentum soil like a collapsing sand castle.

t_ch: 怎么了，你想让我吃这个？这炒饭看着有点不妙啊，你看这颜色…呃，你非要我吃了这个不成——
t_en: What, you want me to eat this? This fried rice looks a bit nasty... Just look at this color. Um, you really want me to eat it...?

t_ch: 等、等会！你别往我嘴里塞啊呜咳咳咳……
t_en: W—Wait! Don't shove it in my mouth argh... *cough*

t_ch: 咳…你突然干啥啊！哎呦喂…真是噎死我了！
t_en: *cough* What are you doing all of a sudden! Ouch... That almost choked me!

t_ch: 不过你这炒饭…啥味道也没有啊？倒也没看起来那么难吃，但口感跟矿渣似的……
t_en: But this fried rice... It doesn't taste like anything. It ain't as bad as it looks, but it feels like eating mine dust...

t_ch: 我就说你吃不了吧。
t_en: I told you that you can't eat.

t_ch: 意识到问题了吗？
t_en: Do you see the problem now?

t_ch: 难道是我舌头不灵了？咋回事，我不明白啊……
t_en: Did something happen to my tongue? What's going on? I don't get it...

t_ch: 你失去了物理意义上的身体。
t_en: You have lost the physical body forever.

t_ch: 你已经变成了一只幽灵…
t_en: You've turned into a ghost...

t_ch: 这么说…是我已经死了呗？
t_en: So... I'm dead?

t_ch: 那我为啥还能和你说话呢？难不成你也……
t_en: Then how come I can still talk to you? Are you also...

t_ch: 你只是裂界模拟诞生的产物。
t_en: You're just a creation emulated by the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 这话不太中听啊…
t_en: That's a bit rude...

t_ch: 哎，行吧。虽然我不太懂你说的那什么「裂界」到底是啥，但我好像也稍微理解了……
t_en: Yeah, alright. I don't really get what's this Fragmentum thing you talk about, but I think I understand...

t_ch: 这么看来…我岂不是吃不了好吃的了吗？那也太空虚了吧……
t_en: So... I can't eat tasty food anymore? That's really sad...

t_ch: 你看着面前的男人露出寂寞的神情，随后便如溃散的沙塔般于裂界的土地溶解。
t_en: The man before you shows a lonely expression before dissolving back into the Fragmentum soil like a collapsing sand castle.

t_ch: 你好呀，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！能…能请你为我拍张照吗？
t_en: Hello! C—Can you take a picture of me?

t_ch: 你在和我说话吗？
t_en: Are you talking to me?

t_ch: 对呀，这附近也没别人啦。
t_en: Yep. There's no one else around.

t_ch: 行啊，但怎么这么突然？
t_en: Sure. Why though?

t_ch: 其实今天是我的生日！娜塔莎姐姐想为我拍张照，然后再寄给爸爸妈妈，让他们看看我的变化！
t_en: It's actually my birthday today! Miss Natasha wants to take a photo of me and mail it to my Mom and Dad, so they can see how much I've grown!

t_ch: 不过，我怎么找也找不到娜塔莎姐姐…正好{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}你路过，我就想能不能问问看，嘿嘿。
t_en: But I can't find Miss Natasha at all... Then you passed by and I thought maybe I can ask you, hehe.

t_ch: 那你摆个姿势吧，要拍了哦——
t_en: Well, do a pose then. Here I go~

t_ch: 好耶！谢谢{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！
t_en: Yay! Thank you!

t_ch: 拍好了吗！快让我看看——
t_en: Did you take it? Let me see!

t_ch: …咦？这照片上怎么什么都没有哇。
t_en: ...Huh? Why is there nothing in this picture?

t_ch: 大概因为你只是裂界的残影吧…
t_en: Probably because you're only an echo of the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 裂界…是大人说的那个，很危险的地方吗？
t_en: Fragmentum... Is that the very dangerous place the adults talk about?

t_ch: 难道说——我变成裂界里的怪物了吗？
t_en: Did I... become a Fragmentum monster?

t_ch: 是的。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 不，你更像是幽灵。
t_en: No, you are more like a ghost.

t_ch: 哦！原来如此。
t_en: Oh! I get it.

t_ch: 但就算这样，应该也能被相机拍到吧？
t_en: But I should still show up in photographs.

t_ch: 唔，我想这应该不是{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}的技术问题…我再拜托别人帮我拍一张就好！
t_en: Mmm, it's probably not because of your photography skills... I'll ask someone else to take one for me!

t_ch: 是我拍照技术不行，对不起…
t_en: It's because of my bad photography skills. Sorry...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，没事的！我再拜托别人帮我拍一张就好。
t_en: Hehe, it's okay! I'll just ask someone else to take one for me!

t_ch: 总之——谢谢你啦，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！
t_en: Thank you anyways!

t_ch: 留下一张灿烂的笑脸，这位乖巧懂事的孩子便散发黯淡的光芒，在裂界的街道中散去。
t_en: This nice and polite child puts on a bright smile as their figure radiates a dim light, then disappears from the streets constructed by the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 拍好了吗！快让我看看——
t_en: Did you take it? Let me see!

t_ch: …咦？{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}…是没拍到我吗？照片里怎么一个人都没有啊。
t_en: ...Huh? Did you... not take my picture? Why is there no one in it?

t_ch: 可能因为你只是裂界的残影…
t_en: Maybe because you're only an echo of the Fragmentum...

t_ch: 裂界…是大人说的那个，很危险的地方吗？
t_en: Fragmentum... Is that the very dangerous place the adults talk about?

t_ch: 难道说——我变成裂界里的怪物了吗？但不对呀，照片里好像不是我站着的地方……
t_en: Did I... become a Fragmentum monster? No. This place in the photo isn't where I was...

t_ch: 是的。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 不，你更像是幽灵。
t_en: No, you are more like a ghost.

t_ch: 哦！原来如此。
t_en: Oh! I get it.

t_ch: 但就算这样，应该也能被相机拍到吧？
t_en: But I should still show up in photographs.

t_ch: 唔，我想这应该不是{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}的技术问题…我再拜托别人帮我拍一张就好！
t_en: Mmm, it's probably not because of your photography skills... I'll ask someone else to take one for me!

t_ch: 是我拍照技术不行，对不起…
t_en: It's because of my bad photography skills. Sorry...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，没事的！我再拜托别人帮我拍一张就好。
t_en: Hehe, it's okay! I'll just ask someone else to take one for me!

t_ch: 总之——谢谢你啦，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！
t_en: Thank you anyways!

t_ch: 留下一张灿烂的笑脸，这位乖巧懂事的孩子便散发黯淡的光芒，在裂界的街道中散去。
t_en: This nice and polite child puts on a bright smile as their figure radiates a dim light, then disappears from the streets constructed by the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 在你眼前的这名男子看似有些不大自然。他的神情局促不安，目光游离，却又总是盯着你那鼓鼓囊囊的口袋不放。
t_en: The man before you seems rather awkward. His expression is unsettled and nervous. His eyes dart everywhere, yet always return to your bulging pockets.

t_ch: 你很快明白，该男子恐怕是一名小偷。你莫名有些气恼，却又感到一丝可怜——想必他绝不知道你那口袋里装的都是些什么东西。
t_en: You soon realize this man is probably a thief. You feel upset, but also a bit sorry for him — He probably has no idea what items you are carrying.

t_ch: …喂！{M#你小子}{F#你这小姑娘}，看什么看！有啥好看的！
t_en: ...Hey! {M#Boy}{F#Girl}, what are you looking at? Nothing to see here!

t_ch: 看你比较可疑。
t_en: I'm looking at you being suspicious.

t_ch: 哈？可疑？你少看不起人了！不就是看我像个小偷……
t_en: Huh? Suspicious? You're just looking down on me! You just think I look like a thief...

t_ch: …啧，说漏嘴了！
t_en: ...Tch, slip of the tongue.

t_ch: 看你比较帅。
t_en: I'm looking at you being handsome.

t_ch: 你…你还敢阴阳怪气我！不就是看我像个小偷……
t_en: Y—You dare mock me? You just think I look like a thief...

t_ch: …啧，说漏嘴了！
t_en: ...Tch, slip of the tongue.

t_ch: 我就看你，怎么了？
t_en: I just want to look at you. What about it?

t_ch: 你…你敢挑衅我？！本想顺点东西神不知鬼不觉地走人…看来这下非得来硬的不可了哈。
t_en: Y—You're taunting me!? I wanted to swipe something quick then sneak away... But now I have to get tough with you.

t_ch: 兄弟们，跟我一起上！千万别让他向铁卫报了案！
t_en: Come with me, friends! Don't let this one report us to the Guards!

t_ch: 咳、咳咳…就是想挣点钱养家糊口，没想到还挨一顿毒打…{M#大哥}{F#大姐}您真不是人哪！
t_en: *cough*... I just wanted to get some money to keep myself fed, but now I'm all beaten up... {M#Sir}{F#Ma'am}, you're really violent!

t_ch: 想挣钱就找份正经工作。
t_en: If you want money, go find a proper job.

t_ch: 合法…哎，哪儿有那么简单啊。
t_en: A proper job... Heh. Easier said than done.

t_ch: 这贝洛伯格刚打完仗不久，墙外又是大风大雪…挣钱可太难了啊。
t_en: Belobog just finished fighting the war, and the outside is all snow and blizzard... It's so tough to earn money.

t_ch: 我这也是迫不得已…！要只养活我一人也就罢了——谁叫我当时赌气离家出走，现在又对我那老爹实在过意不去…我得想办法补偿他哪，唉。
t_en: I have no other way! It would have been okay if I just needed to look after myself, but I ran away from home and now feel bad for my Dad... I need to repay him somehow.

t_ch: 哎…不过您也教训的是，我还是找份正经工作吧。从前我总想着这样来钱快，但说到底也是克里珀堡上走钢丝…不知道哪天就把自己赔进去了啊。
t_en: But what you say is right. I should find a proper job. I used to think stealing would make a quick buck, but it's like walking on a tightrope on top of Qlipoth Fort... I'll end up falling to my doom sooner or later.

t_ch: 离家出走？
t_en: Ran away from home?

t_ch: 是啊。当年我年轻气盛，总觉得自己一定能在法务大臣手下谋高就，可我老爹不这么觉得。结果…我俩就闹掰了，老死不相往来。
t_en: Yeah. I was young, and was convinced I could get a great job under the Minister of Justice, but my Dad disagreed. Then... Then we fought and didn't speak to each other again.

t_ch: 后来我才发现老爹他说得对啊——我根本就不是那块料。但活人总不能让饿死吧？我就四处打听求人想混口饭吃……
t_en: Then I realized Dad was right — I'm never gonna be good enough for those kinds of jobs. But I can't just starve, can I? So I begged and asked everyone to give me a job...

t_ch: 可这世道实在是太难了，根本找不到合适的工作…实在没办法，我这才做了扒手，每个月再以「贝洛伯格供暖」员工的名义给老爹寄去生活费。
t_en: But the world is way too hard. I couldn't find a suitable job at all... So, I had to become a thief. I mail my Dad some money every month under the pretense of working for Belobog's heating system.

t_ch: 一开始我也有心理负担，总想着当扒手实在是不光彩。可生活所迫，我竟也慢慢地习惯了…哎。
t_en: I did feel bad at first, and I felt it was dishonorable to be a thief. But I had bills to pay and I got used to this life... *sigh*.

t_ch: 纸是包不住火的。
t_en: You can't lie forever.

t_ch: 您说得对啊，我不能再这样下去了——这要让老爹知道了，那我还有什么脸面对他……
t_en: You are right. I can't continue like this anymore — If my Dad ever finds out, I would never be able to face him again.

t_ch: 还是好好改造，重新做人吧。
t_en: Leave the past behind and start over.

t_ch: 一定、一定！我不能再这样下去了——这要让老爹知道了，那我还有什么脸面对他……
t_en: Definitely! I can't continue like this anymore — If my Dad ever finds out, I would never be able to face him again.

t_ch: 那你好好改造，重新做人。
t_en: Then leave the past behind and start over.

t_ch: 一定、一定！我不能再这样下去了——这要让老爹知道了，那我还有什么脸面对他……
t_en: Definitely! I can't continue like this anymore — If my Dad ever finds out, I would never be able to face him again.

t_ch: 你还敢给我嘴硬？
t_en: You have the guts to try justifying your actions?

t_ch: 我、我啥也没说！不敢了，我下次真不敢了！我保证！我一定找份正经工作，绝不再做这种坑蒙拐骗的事了……
t_en: I—I didn't say anything! I won't... I won't do it again! I promise! I'll go get a proper job, I'll never steal or rob or lie ever again...

t_ch: 面前的男子颓丧地垂着头，又恼又恨地抽泣了起来。
t_en: The man before you lowers his head in defeat, and starts to sob in anger and frustration.

t_ch: 不多时，那嘶哑的啜泣声便同男子的身躯一同消散了。
t_en: After a while, the hoarse sobbing dissipates together with the man's form.

t_ch: 啧啧…前线的数字不容乐观啊。要是能再发明些什么东西，降低伤亡率就好了……
t_en: The casualty figures are discouraging. If only we could invent something to reduce the casualty rate...

t_ch: 哈！大兵，你好！我正在寻找新发明的灵感呢。刚没注意到你，实在不好意思啊。
t_en: Hello, soldier! I'm looking for inspiration for a new invention. I'm sorry I didn't notice you earlier.

t_ch: 所以…你来找我有什么事吗？如果不是太紧急的事，还请麻烦你整理成书面形式交给我的副手，就说是提交给理查德•巴拉金的……
t_en: So... What's the matter? If it is not too urgent, please submit a written report to my deputy and say it's for Richard Balakin.

t_ch: 哦？这难道是你的设计图纸？介意让我观摩观摩吗，大兵？
t_en: Oh, are these your design blueprints? Mind if I have a look, soldier?

t_ch: 这东西…是叫「生命传输器」吗？真是了不起……
t_en: Is this thing called... Life Transmitter? What an amazing invention...

t_ch: 要是有了这个，战场上应急抢救的存活率说不定能翻倍。而且也可以在野战医院里推广……
t_en: With this, the survival rate for emergency treatments on the battlefield could be doubled. And it would also be used in field hospitals...

t_ch: 太好了、太好了！大兵，你可是为我们铁卫立了大功啊！
t_en: Nice! Soldier, you have done us a great service!

t_ch: 想要复现你的图纸应该不难…如果真能造出来，铁卫全军都会记住你的名字的！哈哈哈……
t_en: It shouldn't be difficult to build it from your blueprints. If we succeed, you will be a hero to the Guards! Hahaha...

t_ch: 面前的铁卫如获至宝，仰天大笑。你仿佛能从那严密的铁盔下看到他激动的神情。
t_en: The guard standing in front of you laughs heartily as if he has found a treasure. You can almost see his excited expression through his tightly secured iron helmet.

t_ch: 他踱着步，一面思考着技术图纸的实现方案，一面向残响回廊深处走去，直到他来时的踪迹彻底消失——就像他从未出现在这里一般。
t_en: As he thinks about how to materialize the plans in the blueprints, he paces towards the depths of the Corridor of Fading Echoes until the traces of his appearance completely dissipate — as if he had never been here.

t_ch: 哦？这难道是你的设计图纸？介意让我观摩观摩吗，大兵？
t_en: Oh, are these your design blueprints? Mind if I have a look, soldier?

t_ch: 这东西…是叫「简易护具」吗？不错，不错……
t_en: Is this thing called... Simple Protective Gear? Hmm, not bad...

t_ch: …不过，很可惜——你的想法很好，但这套护具的防护效率似乎并不如我们现役的制服。如果把它强行挂在制服外面又有可能限制行动……
t_en: Your idea is good, but... this gear doesn't seem to be as protective as our current uniform. And if we wear it outside the uniform, it may restrict our movement.

t_ch: 嗯…无论如何，我会顺着你的思路继续往下想的。感谢你提供宝贵建议，大兵。
t_en: Hmm... Anyway, I'll keep thinking along these lines. Thank you for your valuable advice, soldier.

t_ch: 面前的铁卫手扶脑袋作思考状。你仿佛能从那严密的铁盔下看到他虬曲的眉头。
t_en: The guard in front of you puts his hand on his head in thought. You fancy you can see his deep frown even under his full-face helmet.

t_ch: 他踱着步，一面思考着技术图纸的实现方案，一面向残响回廊深处走去，直到他来时的踪迹彻底消失——就像他从未出现在这里一般。
t_en: As he thinks about how to materialize the plans in the blueprints, he paces towards the depths of the Corridor of Fading Echoes until the traces of his appearance completely dissipate — as if he had never been here.

t_ch: 哦？这难道是你的设计图纸？介意让我观摩观摩吗，大兵？
t_en: Oh, are these your design blueprints? Mind if I have a look, soldier?

t_ch: 这东西…是叫「迷彩油漆」吗？哈哈，有点意思……
t_en: Is this thing called... Camo Paint? Haha, interesting...

t_ch: 你是说…只要把这玩意泼在兄弟们身上，那帮怪物就看不到我们了，对吗？是个有趣的想法，不过那帮怪物是否靠视力索敌还是个问题……
t_en: So, you're saying that if we are covered with this stuff, the monsters won't be able to see us, right? Interesting stuff, but we don't know for sure if the monsters are using their sight to locate enemies...

t_ch: 但我个人觉得这是个非常有意思的发明——我会顺着你的思路想想看，是否有复现的可能…感谢你提供宝贵建议，大兵！
t_en: But I personally think it's a very interesting invention, and I will follow your line of thinking and see if it can be made... Thank you for your valuable advice, soldier!

t_ch: 面前的铁卫手扶脑袋作思考状。你仿佛能从那严密的铁盔下看到他虬曲的眉头。
t_en: The guard in front of you puts his hand on his head in thought. You fancy you can see his deep frown even under his full-face helmet.

t_ch: 他踱着步，一面思考着技术图纸的实现方案，一面向残响回廊深处走去，直到他来时的踪迹彻底消失——就像他从未出现在这里一般。
t_en: As he thinks about how to materialize the plans in the blueprints, he paces towards the depths of the Corridor of Fading Echoes until the traces of his appearance completely dissipate — as if he had never been here.

t_ch: 哦？这难道是你的设计图纸？介意让我观摩观摩吗，大兵？
t_en: Oh, are these your design blueprints? Mind if I have a look, soldier?

t_ch: 这东西…是叫「愈合喷剂」吗？真是不可思议的想法……
t_en: Is this thing called... Healing Spray? What an incredible idea...

t_ch: 要是有了这个，兄弟们就能在战场上快速处理伤口，而且也比我们以往的方式更加安全……
t_en: With this, our wounds can be quickly taken care of on the battlefield, and it's also safer than our previous methods...

t_ch: 只是…你的设计图纸中提及的材料似乎很难获取。但如果真能造出来，铁卫全军都会记住你的名字的！哈哈哈……
t_en: It's just... the materials mentioned in your blueprints seem to be hard to come by. But if you do manage to make it, then all of the Guards will remember your name! Ahahaha...

t_ch: 面前的铁卫如获至宝，仰天大笑。你仿佛能从那严密的铁盔下看到他激动的神情。
t_en: The guard standing in front of you laughs heartily as if he has found a treasure. You can almost see his excited expression through his tightly secured iron helmet.

t_ch: 他踱着步，一面思考着技术图纸的实现方案，一面向残响回廊深处走去，直到他来时的踪迹彻底消失——就像他从未出现在这里一般。
t_en: As he thinks about how to materialize the plans in the blueprints, he paces towards the depths of the Corridor of Fading Echoes until the traces of his appearance completely dissipate — as if he had never been here.

t_ch: 哦？这难道是你的设计图纸？介意让我观摩观摩吗，大兵？
t_en: Oh, are these your design blueprints? Mind if I have a look, soldier?

t_ch: 这东西…是叫「科技护具」吗？真是叹为观止的创意……
t_en: Is this thing called... High-Tech Protective Gear? What an astounding idea...

t_ch: 据我目测，这套护具的防护效率应该远高于我们现有的制服…可能有两倍不止！
t_en: As far as I can tell, the protection offered by this gear is much higher than our current uniform... Maybe twice as much!

t_ch: 只是…你的设计图纸中提及的材料似乎很难获取。但如果真能造出来，铁卫全军都会记住你的名字的！哈哈哈……
t_en: It's just... the materials mentioned in your blueprints seem to be hard to come by. But if you do manage to make it, then all of the Guards will remember your name! Ahahaha...

t_ch: 面前的铁卫如获至宝，仰天大笑。你仿佛能从那严密的铁盔下看到他激动的神情。
t_en: The guard standing in front of you laughs heartily as if he has found a treasure. You can almost see his excited expression through his tightly secured iron helmet.

t_ch: 他踱着步，一面思考着技术图纸的实现方案，一面向残响回廊深处走去，直到他来时的踪迹彻底消失——就像他从未出现在这里一般。
t_en: As he thinks about how to materialize the plans in the blueprints, he paces towards the depths of the Corridor of Fading Echoes until the traces of his appearance completely dissipate — as if he had never been here.

t_ch: 啊啊…不好意思，请问你能帮我修理一下这个屏障发生器吗？
t_en: Ahh... Excuse me, could you help me fix this Barrier Generator?

t_ch: 它好像…启、启动不了了！我检查了一遍，似乎是线路出了点问题…但我不知道该怎么修好它……
t_en: I can't turn it on... There seems to be a problem with the wiring... But I don't know how to fix it...

t_ch: 我…我其实是昨天才上前线的，所以对这东西还不了解…所以，能请你帮忙看看吗？
t_en: I... only came to the front lines yesterday. I still don't know much about the Barrier Launcher... Can you please take a look at it?

t_ch: 听说有大批怪物正向这里靠近…如果不修好它的话，我们就全完了…！
t_en: I heard that a large number of monsters are approaching here... If we can't fix it, we're all going to die!

t_ch: 我来帮你看看。
t_en: Let me have a look.

t_ch: 谢、谢谢…！
t_en: Th—Thank you!

t_ch: 啊…好、好了！多谢，多谢！
t_en: Ahh... It's fixed! Thank you so much!

t_ch: 这下屏障就启动了…至少能抵挡它们一段时间！我得赶紧去疏散平民了——你也保重…你是个好人，可千万别死了啊！
t_en: The Barrier is activated... It can at least fend off those monsters for a while! I have to evacuate the civilians... You look after yourself. We can't have good folks like you die!

t_ch: 愿贝洛伯格长存！愿大守护者长存…！
t_en: May Belobog stand forever! May the Supreme Guardian live forever!

t_ch: 这名局促的新兵颤抖地向你鞠躬致谢后便奔向了堡垒深处——你远远望见那瘦弱的身躯在冰冷的裂界中化作了残光。
t_en: The ill-at-ease recruit gives you a bow of thanks despite his shaking body, then rushes towards the depths of the fort. You watch from a distance as his skinny form dissolves into broken light in the freezing Fragmentum.

t_ch: 啊…有谁在那儿吗？是你吗，队长……
t_en: Ahh... Who is there? Is it you, captain?

t_ch: 我…我看不清东西了…是不是我的头盔…被什么玩意糊住了…？
t_en: I can't see anything... Is my helmet covered with something?

t_ch: 我…我的腿在哪儿…我感觉不到——呕……
t_en: Where... are my legs? I can't feel... *barfs*...

t_ch: 一名虚弱的铁卫倚在墙边，似乎大限已至。如果裂界也能复制现实中的气味，那么在刚才那阵凄惨的呕吐声后，你应当闻到了浓郁的铁锈味。
t_en: A guard leans against the wall, looking as if his time had come. If the Fragmentum can replicate scents, then you should have smelled the thick tang of iron after that wretched barf.

t_ch: 你别说话了，坚持住！
t_en: Stop talking! Hang in there!

t_ch: 队长…我…知道了…我的时候到了……
t_en: Captain... I... I know my time has come...

t_ch: 能告诉我…我打得怎么样吗？
t_en: Can you tell me... How was I on the battlefield?

t_ch: 你打得很勇敢。
t_en: You were brave.

t_ch: 勇敢…我的爸爸…妈妈…会为我骄傲…吗……
t_en: Brave... My father and... mother... will they be proud of me?

t_ch: 只是…我…坚持不了了…回不去了……
t_en: I'll never know... I can't... go back now...

t_ch: 对不…起……
t_en: I... I'm sorry...

t_ch: 别说傻话了，你没事的！
t_en: Don't be silly. You will be fine!

t_ch: 哈哈…这时候就…别开玩笑了……
t_en: Haha... Another joke of yours...

t_ch: 只是…我…坚持不了了……
t_en: I... I can't...

t_ch: 对不…起……
t_en: I... I'm sorry...

t_ch: （沉默地看着她）
t_en: (Look at him in silence.)

t_ch: 队长…队长……
t_en: Captain... captain...

t_ch: 声音…我听不见声音了…你们在哪儿……
t_en: Voice... I can't hear your voice... Where are you?

t_ch: 我…坚持不了了……
t_en: I... can't hold on anymore...

t_ch: 对不…起……
t_en: I... I'm sorry...

t_ch: 可怜的铁卫不再出声。你将她上身扶起，轻柔地落在地面上，又将那柄久经沙场的钢枪放入她的怀中，试图以符合战士身份的、体面的葬礼为她送行。
t_en: The poor guard stops making a sound. You gently place his body into a sitting position and put his battle-hardened spear into his arms, trying to give him a respectable funeral worthy of a warrior.

t_ch: 你以自己认知中最合乎礼仪的敬礼姿势向她致敬。仿佛是对你敬意的回应，她那裂界构成的脆弱身躯顷刻间纷飞四散，归于尘土。
t_en: You salute him in the most ceremonial way you know. As if in response to you, his body, made by the Fragmentum, shatters and returns to dust in an instant.

t_ch: 看你好像很发愁的样子。
t_en: It seems like something's bothering you.

t_ch: 丹鼎司洞天重开之后，虽然人来人往的程度不及灾前，但这里多少也算是鱼龙混杂。
t_en: After the Alchemy Commission delve was reopened, it has become less busy than before the disaster, but it is still a mixed bag.

t_ch: 你有没有听说过，有一类化外民组织叫作「放生帮」？叫他们为组织也有些奇怪，我听说有些人是抱团行动，有些人则纯粹是自发行为……
t_en: Have you heard of an Outworlder organization called "The Unshackled"? It might not be suitable to call them an organization, as while some of them act as a group, others operate alone...

t_ch: 没听说过什么「放生帮」…
t_en: I've never heard of The Unshackled...

t_ch: 说是「通过放生来积累功德」的一群人，这都不重要……
t_en: It is said to be a group of people who accumulate merits by releasing living creatures, but that's not the point...

t_ch: 「放生帮」行事，我略有耳闻。
t_en: I've heard a little about The Unshackled.

t_ch: 你听说过就好办了，这帮人既固执、又愚昧，简直不像接受过现代教育的人。
t_en: It's great you've heard of them. They are an ignorant and stubborn bunch devoid of any proper education.

t_ch: 他们什么都往波月古海里放生，那些放生「鳞渊冰泉」的人姑且还算在法律边缘徘徊，但是放生野生生物的就属于违返仙舟律法了。
t_en: They throw whatever they get their hands on into the Lunarescent Depths. Pouring Scalegorge Spring Water might not be against Xianzhou's laws, but releasing wild creatures undoubtedly is.

t_ch: 前段时间，我抓住了不少「放生帮」放生的现行，他们好像已经盯上我了。我去海岸巡逻期间，他们假装老实；一旦我该休息了……
t_en: Recently, I caught many of them red-handed during their acts of "liberation," and I think they have me on their radar. They have been on their best behavior when I'm patrolling the coast. Once I take a break, however...

t_ch: 他们又开始放生了。
t_en: They go back to their old ways again.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That's right.

t_ch: 他们也开始休息了。
t_en: They take a break too.

t_ch: 不对，他们就出来开始放生了。
t_en: No, they go back to their old act.

t_ch: 总之，我想有个人在我休息的时候帮我在海滩上走一下就好了。要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下…你说这样的人哪里找呢？
t_en: Anyway, I need someone to help me watch over the coast when I'm on break. If any of The Unshackled's members are sighted, my replacement needs to warn them and deal with whatever they release... Do you have any suitable candidates in mind?

t_ch: 我不就是吗？
t_en: Aren't I the person you're looking for?

t_ch: …有种跑腿的预感。
t_en: ...Sounds like a menial job to me.

t_ch: 放心，护珠人是不会亏待你的，就拜托你了。
t_en: Don't worry. I, as a Pearlkeeper, will reward you handsomely. I'll count on you then!

t_ch: （这就是跃渊巡逻的区域，<color=#dbc291ff>要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下</color>…真是的，守护这片沙滩的人到底是谁呢？）
t_en: (This is Yueyuan's patrol area. <color=#dbc291ff>Drive away The Unshackled and take care of the things they release</color>... Honestly, who is protecting this beach?)

t_ch: （这好像是…漂流瓶？波月古海也存在着洋流吗？漂流瓶又是从哪里来的？）
t_en: (Is this a... drift bottle? Is there an ocean current in the Lunarescent Depths? Where did this drift bottle come from?)

t_ch: （总之先打开看看，再考虑怎么办吧。）
t_en: (Let's take a look for now and decide what to do from there.)

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了。总之，先把求救信交给跃渊处理吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening on the beach. I should hand this letter over to Yueyuan.)

t_ch: （将捡到的漂流瓶交给跃渊）
t_en: (You hand the drift bottle you picked up to Yueyuan)

t_ch: 说老实话，我看这漂流瓶里字迹工整，我怀疑这是有人整蛊…不过，为了安全起见，我会安排人手驾驶星槎前往波月古海搜救的。
t_en: Frankly speaking, from the neat handwriting in this letter, I suspect this is just another prank... But, to err on the safe side, I'll send someone to the Lunarescent Depths on a rescue mission via a starskiff.

t_ch: 不过，我也建议你不必抱太大希望。鳞渊境存在着特殊的洞天技术，别说是落难者，就连我们也无法前往古海深处，这漂流瓶造假的可能性极高。
t_en: Still, please don't expect too much from this operation. Due to the special technologies in Scalegorge Waterscape delve, not even we can reach the depths, let alone the castaway. This makes me highly suspect that this letter is a fake.

t_ch: 但不能因此放弃调查，对吧？
t_en: But we cannot give up on our investigation because of that, can we?

t_ch: 你说的没错，就算可能性再低，我们也会到海上调查一番的。
t_en: You're right. No matter how slim the chance is, we still have to go out on the water and conduct an investigation.

t_ch: 这会不会是放生帮干的呢？
t_en: Do you think this could be the work of The Unshackled?

t_ch: …倒是不能排除这样的可能性，他们的话，确实会干出浪费公共资源的事情。
t_en: ...We can't rule out that possibility yet. Doing things that waste public resources is very much something they might do.

t_ch: 总之，辛苦你了，这是你应得的报酬。
t_en: Either way, thank you for your hard work. Here are your well-deserved rewards.

t_ch: 听说你找我帮忙巡逻。
t_en: I hear you needed me for a patrol?

t_ch: 对了，不知道<color=#dbc291ff>漂流瓶的事</color>你还记得多少？我们护珠人派出了一队搜救人员，到波月古海上搜了一圈什么都没有找到。
t_en: By the way, do you still remember that thing with the <color=#dbc291ff>drift bottle</color>? We dispatched a rescue team to the Lunarescent Depths but found nothing.

t_ch: 你放心，我对你自然是信任有加，但是这漂流瓶的来历实在太怪了，越来越像整蛊了。
t_en: Fret not, you have my trust, but this drift bottle here is just too odd. The more I look at it, the more it seems like a prank to me.

t_ch: 如果你发现了类似的情况，<color=#dbc291ff>你可以自己处理掉，或者你不放心的话，仍然可以交给我们。</color>
t_en: If you come across any similar circumstances, <color=#dbc291ff>you can take care of them yourself, or hand them over to us if you're feeling uneasy.</color>

t_ch: 总之，麻烦你去海滩上走一遭了。
t_en: Either way, please do another patrol across the beach for me.

t_ch: （这就是跃渊巡逻的区域，<color=#dbc291ff>要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下</color>…真是的，守护这片沙滩的人到底是谁呢？）
t_en: (This is Yueyuan's patrol area. <color=#dbc291ff>Drive away The Unshackled and take care of the things they release</color>... Honestly, who is protecting this beach?)

t_ch: 这毫无疑问是一个漂流瓶。
t_en: This is undoubtedly a drift bottle.

t_ch: （总之先打开看看，再考虑怎么办吧。）
t_en: (Let's take a look for now and decide what to do from there.)

t_ch: （落难者好像已经度过了最初的危机，这个漂流瓶应该怎么办呢？）
t_en: (The castaway seems to have overcome their initial hardships. What should I do with this drift bottle?)

t_ch: 写点什么，然后扔回海里。
t_en: I'll write something in reply and throw it back.

t_ch: 还是把漂流瓶交给跃渊吧。
t_en: I should hand the drift bottle over to Yueyuan.

t_ch: （既然决定将漂流瓶扔回去，还是装点什么进去吧……）
t_en: (Since I have decided to throw the drift bottle back, I should probably put something in...)

t_ch: 给予帮助以外的一切支持。
t_en: Offer all the support I can, except actual help.

t_ch: 「你在那边也要好好吃饭，好好睡觉，一个人也要照顾好自己。人生路只能自己走，孤独是常态，你要学会习惯……」
t_en: "I hope you're eating and sleeping well over there. Even though you are alone, you should take good care of yourself. You are in charge of your life, and you need to get used to being alone..."

t_ch: 你写了一封感人至深的书信，教会了一个人如何在困境中汲取力量。有时候，一句打动人心的话语，能远远胜过物质上的援助。
t_en: You write an inspiring letter about harnessing power from adversity. Sometimes, a heartfelt word can be far more impactful than material assistance.

t_ch: 一些生存指南小贴士。
t_en: Give some survival tips.

t_ch: 「你千万要远离野兽出没的地方，否则会被马上吃掉。要是不会钻木取火的话，你可以试试打火机，一定要有耐心……」
t_en: "Please stay away from places where wild animals are active or you will be eaten by them. If you don't know how to start a fire, you can try using a lighter. It is important to remain patient at all times..."

t_ch: 你总结了一份荒野求生的知识和技巧，教会了一个人如何免受恶劣天气和野生动物的侵害。其中最重要的是，落难人也不可以破坏植被和野生动物栖息地！
t_en: You compile a list of knowledge and tips for surviving in the wilderness, teaching them to protect themselves from harsh weather and wild animals. Most importantly, you warn the castaway against destroying the habitat of the flora and fauna on the island!

t_ch: 护珠人上一次的搜查结果。
t_en: The result of the last search by the Pearlkeepers.

t_ch: 「护珠人派出了一队搜救人员，到波月古海上搜了一圈什么都没有找到，你到底在哪里……」
t_en: "The Pearlkeepers dispatched a search and rescue team to explore the entire Lunarescent Depths, but they found nothing. Where exactly is your location..."

t_ch: 你客观陈述了护珠人为了寻找失事者而付出的努力，并且强力建议落难者给出自己的具体位置，以提高获救概率。
t_en: You objectively stated the efforts that the Pearlkeepers have gone to in order to find the castaway and strongly suggested they provide their precise location to increase their chances of rescue.

t_ch: 你将漂流瓶扔了回去。按照常识来看，漂流瓶多半不会回到落难者手中吧……
t_en: You threw the drift bottle back. Logically speaking, there should be no way it makes it back to the castaway...

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: （将捡到的漂流瓶交给跃渊）
t_en: (You hand the drift bottle you picked up to Yueyuan)

t_ch: 又是一个漂流瓶？如果说这是一场闹剧的话，未免有些认真。但要我认真对待的话，这封信显得又很荒谬。
t_en: Another drift bottle? This seems a little too committed for a prank. Although, this letter also seems a little absurd to take seriously.

t_ch: 但不能因此放弃调查，对吧？
t_en: But we cannot give up on our investigation because of that, can we?

t_ch: 没错，我们还会再去海上搜索的。
t_en: You're right, we will continue our search at sea.

t_ch: 这会不会是放生帮干的呢？
t_en: Do you think this could be the work of The Unshackled?

t_ch: 虽然听上去挺没有道理的，但这像是他们能做出的事。
t_en: It may sound unlikely, but this does seem like something they could do.

t_ch: 总之，辛苦你了，这是你应得的报酬。
t_en: Either way, thank you for your hard work. Here are your well-deserved rewards.

t_ch: 听说你找我帮忙巡逻。
t_en: I hear you needed me for a patrol?

t_ch: 对了，最近我们又去波月古海上搜索了一番，还没有找到任何落难者有关的情报。
t_en: That's right, we recently went to search the Lunarescent Depths again, but found no information regarding the castaway.

t_ch: 如果说波月古海上真的存在落难者，那落难者肯定是在躲着搜查队…所以，根据我的经验来看，应该这是有人在整蛊。
t_en: If there really is a castaway in the Lunarescent Depths, then they must be actively hiding from the search teams... My experience tells me that this is someone playing a trick.

t_ch: 哎，如果你又发现了类似的情况，<color=#dbc291ff>你可以自己处理掉，或者你不放心的话，仍然可以交给我们。</color>
t_en: Ahh. Well, if you come across anything similar, <color=#dbc291ff>you can deal with it yourself, or hand it over to us, should you feel uneasy.</color>

t_ch: 总之，麻烦你去海滩上走一遭了。
t_en: Either way, please do another patrol across the beach for me.

t_ch: （这就是跃渊巡逻的区域，<color=#dbc291ff>要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下</color>…真是的，守护这片沙滩的人到底是谁呢？）
t_en: (This is Yueyuan's patrol area. <color=#dbc291ff>Drive away The Unshackled and take care of the things they release</color>... Honestly, who is protecting this beach?)

t_ch: 这毫无疑问是一个漂流瓶，还是漂洋过海的那种。
t_en: This is undoubtedly a drift bottle, one that has crossed the ocean, too.

t_ch: （总之先打开看看，再考虑怎么办吧。）
t_en: (Let's take a look for now and decide what to do from there.)

t_ch: （落难者好像已经过上了财务自由的生活，这个漂流瓶应该怎么办呢？）
t_en: (The castaway seems to have made a financially independent life for themselves. What should I do with this drift bottle?)

t_ch: 不管它，直接扔回海里。
t_en: Forget it, just throw it back.

t_ch: 你将漂流瓶扔了回去，谁爱帮忙谁帮。
t_en: You throw the drift bottle back, someone else can deal with that.

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: 还是把信交给阿浮吧。
t_en: I should hand the letter over to Fulie.

t_ch: （万一能打听到落难者的消息呢？）
t_en: (Maybe I can get some information on the castaway?)

t_ch: 还是把漂流瓶交给跃渊吧。
t_en: I should hand the drift bottle over to Yueyuan.

t_ch: （不知道跃渊会作何感受？）
t_en: (I wonder what Yueyuan will think of this?)

t_ch: （将捡到的信交给阿浮）
t_en: (Hand the letter you picked up to Fulie)

t_ch: 这谁呀，随口胡诌是我「儿时玩伴」，这说法就好像我长大了一样！罗浮法律同意了吗？
t_en: Who is this!? Casually talking about being my "childhood playmate" as if I'm old now! Did Luofu law approve of this?

t_ch: 你有没有认识的人落难了？
t_en: Is there anyone you know that has gone missing?

t_ch: 你有没有「现在的玩伴」？
t_en: Do you have any current "playmates"?

t_ch: 没有！绝对没有！这封信也莫名其妙的，不过这个故事我是听人讲起过的。
t_en: No! Absolutely not! This letter makes no sense, but I've heard stories like this before.

t_ch: 「一名海上落难者靠着野外求生的技巧，最后在荒岛上生存下来…」我记得只有我一人对这故事感兴趣，我还说这要真的就有意思了。
t_en: "A person lost at sea uses their wilderness survival skills to survive on a deserted island..." I remember I was the only person who cared about this story. I thought it would be interesting if it really happened.

t_ch: …不过你说这玩意儿是从海里打捞上来的，有点意思。我还是收下来跟邻居家小孩儿炫耀吧，谢谢你啦。
t_en: But you said you salvaged this from the sea? That is interesting. Perhaps I should take this to show off to the neighbor's kid. Thanks!

t_ch: （这是捏造的故事？越来越神秘了……）
t_en: (So it's a made-up story? This keeps getting more and more mysterious...)

t_ch: （将捡到的漂流瓶交给跃渊）
t_en: (You hand the drift bottle you picked up to Yueyuan)

t_ch: 已经过上财务自由的生活了是吧？那我们护珠人也不要管这个人好了！
t_en: They've already made a financially independent life for themselves? Then there's no need for us Pearlkeepers to pay this person any more attention!

t_ch: …你只是生气了，对吧？
t_en: ...You're just angry, aren't you?

t_ch: 这确实是气话…既然落难者还有生还的可能，我们还是会继续搜救的。
t_en: You're right. I didn't mean that... As the castaway may still be alive, we will continue our search.

t_ch: 这会不会是放生帮干的呢？
t_en: Do you think this could be the work of The Unshackled?

t_ch: 越来越有可能了，这故事诡异得离谱……
t_en: It's looking more and more likely. This whole story is very strange...

t_ch: 这也太被动了，既然信上提到了叫作「阿浮」的人，我会抽空问一下的。
t_en: We can't just do nothing! The letter mentions someone named "Fulie," I'll make time to go and ask them about it.

t_ch: 总之，辛苦你了，这是你应得的报酬。
t_en: Either way, thank you for your hard work. Here are your well-deserved rewards.

t_ch: 听说你找我帮忙巡逻。
t_en: I hear you needed me for a patrol?

t_ch: 对了，我已经跟地衡司反映了漂流瓶的情况。
t_en: Ah yes, I have already reported the drift bottle situation to the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 根据地衡司的反馈，他们目前没有收到走失人口的报案，这「落难者」大概率是「虚构情节」，在获得确凿证据之前，护珠人不会再出动海上救援了。
t_en: According to their feedback, they have not received any reports of missing persons, and this "castaway" is likely a fabrication. The Pearlkeepers will not be dispatched for ocean rescue again before obtaining conclusive evidence of their existence.

t_ch: 如果你又发现了类似的情况，<color=#dbc291ff>你就自己处理掉吧。</color>除非，这漂流瓶里存在着确凿的证据。
t_en: If you come across anything similar, <color=#dbc291ff>just deal with it yourself.</color> That is unless you find conclusive evidence inside the drift bottle.

t_ch: 另外，护珠人已经认可了你的能力。<color=#dbc291ff>以后有巡逻任务的时候，你不必专程先来找我，直接去海滩就好，报酬不会少你的。</color>
t_en: Also, the Pearlkeepers have recognized your abilities. <color=#dbc291ff>From now on, you will no longer need to report to me before a patrol. You can go directly to the beach, and you will still be compensated for your efforts.</color>

t_ch: 总之，麻烦你去海滩上走一遭了。
t_en: Either way, please do another patrol across the beach for me.

t_ch: （这就是跃渊巡逻的区域，<color=#dbc291ff>要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下</color>…真是的，守护这片沙滩的人到底是谁呢？）
t_en: (This is Yueyuan's patrol area. <color=#dbc291ff>Drive away The Unshackled and take care of the things they release</color>... Honestly, who is protecting this beach?)

t_ch: 这毫无疑问是一个漂流瓶，还是漂洋过海的那种。
t_en: This is undoubtedly a drift bottle, one that has crossed the ocean, too.

t_ch: （总之先打开看看，再考虑怎么办吧。）
t_en: (Let's take a look for now and decide what to do from there.)

t_ch: （落难者又增加了，还想要回信，这个时候应该怎么办呢？）
t_en: (The ranks of castaways have grown, and they want a reply? What am I supposed to do now?)

t_ch: 不管它，直接扔回海里。
t_en: Forget it. Just throw it back.

t_ch: （这破漂流瓶一直是自说自话，谁知道是真是假呢？）
t_en: (These wacky drift bottles are just talking to themselves. Who knows if what they're saying is true?)

t_ch: 我来写一封回信吧！
t_en: I'll write a reply!

t_ch: （很好，我来给你们讲讲，应不应该回到文明社会！）
t_en: (Alright, let me explain whether you should rejoin civilized society!)

t_ch: 你正打算将漂流瓶扔回海里的时候，突然有个声音叫住了你。
t_en: Just as you are about to cast the drift bottle back into the ocean, a voice calls out to you.

t_ch: 等等！不要扔！
t_en: Stop! Don't throw that!

t_ch: 你是谁，你知道这个漂流瓶？
t_en: Who are you? Do you know about these drift bottles?

t_ch: 我、我我…我只是一个陌生人。我、我不知道什么漂流瓶。
t_en: I, I... I'm just a stranger. I... I don't know about any drift bottles.

t_ch: 我只是在制止你往海里面扔垃圾，你千万住手！
t_en: I am just here to stop you throwing garbage into the ocean. I beg you to stop!

t_ch: 你别管我，我偏要扔进海里！
t_en: Stay out of this. I WILL throw it into the ocean!

t_ch: …千万别，这不是真的从海上漂过来的，你要扔出去，万一回不来了……
t_en: ...Please, don't. This didn't actually float across the ocean. What if you throw it out there and it doesn't come back...

t_ch: 不对不对不对！这漂流瓶就是从海上漂过来的！但是你别扔！
t_en: Wait! I mean! No! This definitely did float across the ocean! But you still can't throw it!

t_ch: 你仔细看过漂流瓶里的信了，你是不是应该回应漂流瓶里的请求呢？
t_en: You've read the drift bottle messages. Do you think you should really be responding to their requests?

t_ch: 漂流瓶里的故事是假的。
t_en: The story in the drift bottle is not true.

t_ch: 这漂流瓶里的信看上去这么假吗？
t_en: Does the letter in the bottle really seem that fake?

t_ch: …等等，我差点就上当了。
t_en: ...Wait, I almost fell for that.

t_ch: 我应该回应漂流瓶的请求。
t_en: I should respond to the request in the drift bottle.

t_ch: …我猜你只是在敷衍我。
t_en: ...You're just humoring me, aren't you?

t_ch: 你还是把漂流瓶还给我吧，我的叙事设计可不能就这样被你破坏了！
t_en: Please just gimme back the bottle. My narrative design cannot be disrupted like this!

t_ch: 海边的陌生人将漂流瓶抱去，就像是祖传下来的宝贝。你眉头一紧，想起自己来到海滩的理由——「放生帮」。
t_en: The stranger from the shore leaves, cradling the drift bottle like a treasured heirloom. You furrow your brow and remember the reason you came to the beach — The Unshackled.

t_ch: 你反正都要扔掉，我拿着怎么了？别盯着我了，马上就走。
t_en: You were going to throw it away anyway, what's wrong with me taking it? Don't stare at me like that, I'm leaving right away.

t_ch: 你是放生什么的？
t_en: What are you unshackling?

t_ch: 既然你已经发现，我就告诉你吧——我放生原创故事！
t_en: Since I've been discovered, I shall tell you — I unshackle original stories!

t_ch: 海边的陌生人笔直地离开了海岸。那天，你仍不知道「放生原创故事」到底是什么意思。
t_en: The stranger from the shore turns and leaves the beach. On that day, you still have no idea just what "unshackling original stories" meant.

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: 你阅读完底稿之后，从字里行间感受到了信件那按耐不住的表达欲——无论是抱怨年轻人在长生社会找不到工作，还是星际和平公司带来的外部压力……
t_en: Upon reading the draft, you sense its uncontrollable urge to express itself: Be it lamenting the fact that young people cannot find jobs within a long-living society, or the external pressure brought about by the Interastral Peace Corporation...

t_ch: 看完之后，别说「星期六」，就连你也不想回到文明社会了，这封信还需要你来添笔？
t_en: After you finish reading it, even you don't wish to return to civilized society, let alone Saturday. Is there anything you can add to this?

t_ch: 你明白了，这位落难者需要的从来不是「陌生人的建议」，而是一种「认同感」。
t_en: You come to understand that what this castaway seeks is never advice from a stranger, but validation and approval.

t_ch: 落难人希望你能作为受苦之人劝劝「星期六」不要回到文明社会。来，写点什么吧。
t_en: The castaway hopes that you, as someone who suffers inside civilized society, can convince Saturday not to return to it. It's time to write something.

t_ch: …但逃避并不能解决问题……
t_en: ...But running away cannot solve anything...

t_ch: 「但是逃避不能解决问题，一时的解脱只会造成长期的痛苦。很多时候，凭一人之力无法改变环境，但是你可以改变自己……」
t_en: "But running away cannot solve anything, as a brief escape will only cause long-term suffering. More often than not, you cannot change the world on your own, but you can change your own perception of it..."

t_ch: 你认为「星期六」不应该逃避责任，应该勇敢地返回现代文明社会之中…至少这里有玉兆、有网络，有着文明社会的宝藏，总比荒岛要好得多。
t_en: You believe that Saturday should not run from their responsibilities, that they should bravely return to civilized society... At least there are jade abaci, the internet, and treasures of modern civilization that far exceed those of life on a deserted island.

t_ch: …但前面只是轻描淡写而已……
t_en: ...That's an understatement, if anything...

t_ch: 「但是前面的惨状不过是轻描淡写。社会人都早早学会了伪装，没有时间留给悲伤，只有表里不一的煎熬——不等你崩溃掉，就立刻会被他人代替……」
t_en: "However, the preceding tragedy is, if anything, an understatement. People in human society have learned to mask their true emotions and have no time to process their sorrows. All there is, is a chain of suffering from the inability to show their true selves... and you'll get replaced by someone else before you've even broken down..."

t_ch: 你的添笔加重了文明社会的危机程度，这封信里根本没有仁义道德——想必「星期六」收到信之后，会横竖睡不着，从字缝里看出「吃人」二字。
t_en: Your additions further exaggerate the critical state of the modern society. This letter contains no shred of kindness or morality. Presumably, Saturday will be unable to sleep after reading this, for the words "naked exploitation" is positively leaping out of the page.

t_ch: …说真的，你们到底在哪里？
t_en: ...Honestly, where are you people?

t_ch: 「护珠人找了你们很多次，你们到底在哪里！请尽量提供有效信息，不要浪费公共资源！」
t_en: "The Pearlkeepers have searched for you many times. Where exactly are you people!? Please provide useful information and stop wasting public resources!"

t_ch: 你根本不在意荒岛上的小小纠结，你关心着如何开展救援。只要这荒岛遇难是真人真事，你所做的就是最正确的判断。
t_en: You don't care one bit about the petty squabbles on the deserted island — you are concerned about how to perform the rescue. If there truly are people in real danger stranded on an island, then this is the correct call to make.

t_ch: 你将漂流瓶扔了回波月古海，希望这些小小建议能够到达所需之人手中。
t_en: You cast the drift bottle back into the Lunarescent Depths, hoping that these suggestions will reach the hands of those that need them.

t_ch: 自然科学的理性不断提醒着你，这封信能回到「护珠人也找不到的荒岛」可能性为零。
t_en: The logic that lies behind science keeps reminding you that the chances of this letter reaching "an island that even the Pearlkeepers could not find" is zero.

t_ch: 但是你做完了你该做的事，这便是意义所在。
t_en: However, you have done what you must. That is what matters.

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: （跃渊说护珠人已经认可了我的能力，我可以独立处理巡逻中产生的情况。）
t_en: (Yueyuan said that the Pearlkeepers have already recognized my talents and that I can deal with anything I encounter on patrol alone.)

t_ch: （<color=#dbc291ff>要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下</color>…今天还会有漂流瓶吗？）
t_en: (<color=#dbc291ff>Drive away The Unshackled and take care of the things they release</color>... I wonder if there will be any drift bottles today?)

t_ch: 这个奇怪的人很有可能是放生帮的一员，作为海滩守护者，你理应好好盘问他一番。
t_en: This weirdo could be a member of The Unshackled... as the Guardian of the Shoreline, you should question him.

t_ch: 喂！
t_en: Hey!

t_ch: 我、我我我什么也没有做！
t_en: I—I—I haven't done anything!

t_ch: 做贼心虚的人都这么说！
t_en: That's exactly what a guilty person would say!

t_ch: 我没有做贼心虚，我只是被你吓到了！没错！
t_en: I'm not guilty, you just startled me! Yeah, that's right!

t_ch: 你背后的酒坛是做什么的？
t_en: What are you doing with those wine jars behind you?

t_ch: 那、那是什么呢？
t_en: Wh—What wine jar?

t_ch: 是我问你问题，还是你问我？
t_en: Are you asking the questions here, or am I?

t_ch: …那是酒坛。
t_en: ...They're wine jars.

t_ch: 好问题，让我求助场外地衡司。
t_en: Good question. Why don't I call a friend in the Realm-Keeping Commission and ask what they think.

t_ch: 别别别，我承认，那就是酒坛。
t_en: No no no stop! You're right, they're wine jars.

t_ch: 这里可不是一般人该来的地方。
t_en: This is no place for regular citizens.

t_ch: 我是为了一件重要的事，非来不可。
t_en: I had to come here. I must do something very important.

t_ch: 不瞒您说，我这身后的酒坛里面装满了五谷精华。仙舟人讲「民以食为天」，这五谷精华赐予了我们体肤、滋润了我们的灵魂和性情。
t_en: I'll be honest, the jars behind me are filled with the essence of grains. The people of Xianzhou believe that food is the paramount necessity of the people, and this essence nourishes our bodies, moisturizes our souls, and shapes our temperament.

t_ch: 如此天地精华，却被囚禁在这口小腹大的坛子里！我决不允许！只要我能将这五谷精华还于天地，那必然是大大大大功德一件啊！
t_en: This miraculous essence has been sealed away inside these tiny vessels! I cannot allow it! If I can just return this essence of grains to the world, it would be a great great GREAT merit!

t_ch: 这么积功攒德之事，{F#女士}{M#先生}不妨与我一同啊！
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, will you not join me on this virtuous mission?

t_ch: 好家伙，可让我抓到放生帮了！
t_en: At long last! I've finally caught an Unshackled in the act!

t_ch: 什么？！你要抓我作甚！
t_en: What!? Why do you want to catch me!?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}辛苦你了，我可得好好教训这放生帮不可。
t_en: Good work, {NICKNAME}. I will teach this Unshackled a good lesson.

t_ch: 你们休想阻止我！
t_en: People like you won't stop me!

t_ch: …你总是在海滩上捡到奇怪的漂流瓶，差点就让我忘了，我们本来是抓放生帮的。
t_en: ...With all those strange drift bottles you kept picking up at the beach, I almost forgot that we were here to catch the Unshackled.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，这是你应得的报酬。
t_en: Good work, here are your well-deserved remuneration.

t_ch: 你和陌生人将粮食精华倒进了波月古海，正好奇着崇高的道德为什么还未投下赞许？
t_en: You and the stranger pour the grain essence into the Lunarescent Depths, and you wonder why the High Morals have not praised you yet.

t_ch: 你想起了在某些世界里，人们将茶叶放生于海中，间接引发了独立战争…任何放生行为都不可以小觑，今日也是一样。
t_en: At that moment, you remember that in a certain world, there were people who released tea leaves into the ocean, which indirectly brought about a war of independence... Any release should not be taken lightly, and today is no exception.

t_ch: 谢谢你，志同道合的朋友。酒坛我会带走，功德会留在心头。
t_en: Thank you, my like-minded friend. I shall take the wine jars with me. Your great merit shall stay in my heart.

t_ch: 我这人记性不好，下次见面的时候，记得和我打招呼，我们一起积攒功德！
t_en: My memory isn't the best, so next time you see me, make sure to say hi and we can continue to collect merit, together!

t_ch: 陌生人迈着欢快的步伐离开了海滩，你做了好人好事，崇高的道德为什么还未投下赞许？
t_en: The stranger happily leaves the beach with a skip in his step. You have done a great deed, so why haven't the High Morals praised you yet?

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: 我要和你一起放生五谷精华！
t_en: I want to release the essence of grains with you!

t_ch: 好好好！你放一坛，我放一坛，我们功德无边！
t_en: Okay, okay! You do one jar, I'll do the other. We'll accumulate boundless merits together!

t_ch: （跃渊说护珠人已经认可了我的能力，我可以独立处理巡逻中产生的情况。）
t_en: (Yueyuan said that the Pearlkeepers have already recognized my talents and that I can deal with anything I encounter on patrol alone.)

t_ch: （<color=#dbc291ff>要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下</color>…今天还会有漂流瓶吗？）
t_en: (<color=#dbc291ff>Drive away The Unshackled and take care of the things they release</color>... I wonder if there will be any drift bottles today?)

t_ch: 海滩上放着一本书，你敏锐地察觉到了这并不对劲。难道这又是放生帮干的好事？「放生知识」——放眼整个放生界，这也是一个全新的课题。
t_en: You find a book on the beach and keenly perceive that something is not right about it. Could this be the work of the Unshackled, too? Unshackling knowledge — Even among the vast realm of unshackling, this is a whole new venture.

t_ch: 你拿起书本，你的手稍微颤动了一下——因为它远比你想象中更重。你知道精装本通常会有硬纸板、织物，甚至是小牛皮革等材料，但是它还是*太重了*。
t_en: You pick up the book, and your hand trembles slightly — it is far heavier than you imagined. Though you understand hardcover book bindings can come in cardboard, fabric, or even leather, this is still TOO heavy for any of them.

t_ch: 说真的，很少有书会在「重量感」这个维度上给人留下如此深刻的印象。既然如此，勉为其难看看它的书名吧。它的书名叫作《前世》。
t_en: Honestly, it is rare that a book leaves such striking impression in its weight. That being the case, you feel you should inspect the title. It is called Previous Reincarnation.

t_ch: 波月古海，持明族，前世…真是意味深长，可是它真的应该这么重吗，要不打开看看吧？
t_en: The Lunarescent Depths, the Vidyadhara, previous reincarnation... Though it is a profound subject, should it really be this heavy? Why not take a look inside?

t_ch: 到底什么是重量级知识啊？
t_en: What kind of knowledge could be this heavy?

t_ch: 你翻开了这本书。书本内部被掏出方格空间，一把漂亮的手枪静静躺在其中。
t_en: As you open the book, you realize that the contents have been hollowed out into a square storage space, where a beautiful pistol lies silently.

t_ch: 我没有什么兴趣，直接带走。
t_en: Not interested. I'll bring it along with me.

t_ch: 是谁在海滩上乱扔垃圾，不讲文明，不树新风。只有你记得保护海滩，人人有责。
t_en: Who would leave trash lying around on the beach like this in such an uncivilized manner? It's almost as if only you remember to take care of the environment and protect the beaches around here.

t_ch: 突然，你的眼前一片漆黑，有人用*宽大的手掌*遮住了你的眼睛。你感觉到脖子旁冰冰凉凉的，毫无疑问那是足以吹毛断发的锐器。
t_en: Suddenly, all you see before you is darkness, as if someone covered your eyes with a *giant hand*. You sense an icy sting on the side of your neck, undoubtedly from a blade sharp enough to split hair.

t_ch: 不要乱动，冷静地听我问完。你，为什么要拿走我的手枪？
t_en: Don't move. Keep quiet and listen to my question. Why are you taking my pistol?

t_ch: 手、手枪？这不是一本书吗？
t_en: P—Pistol!? I thought this was a book?

t_ch: 书里放着一把手枪，你不必假装不知道。
t_en: There is a pistol inside the book. There's no need to pretend you don't know about it.

t_ch: 我不知道，我没想带走你的枪。
t_en: I didn't know. I didn't intend to take your gun.

t_ch: 通常来说，我应该相信你吗？我可不敢。
t_en: You think I should take your word for that? I'm not that naive.

t_ch: 听好了，我的工作内容不太能见光。而如今我打算金盆洗手，将我的「前世」就此放生，好好过我的下半辈子。
t_en: Listen, my job can't really see the light of day. Today, I want to wash my hands of that and release my "previous reincarnation" here, so I can move on to enjoy what I have left.

t_ch: 但是你出现得太不是时候了。无论你是有意还是无意，把手枪交给我，然后闭上眼默数，从1数到<unbreak>100</unbreak>，我保你性命无忧。
t_en: However, you've appeared at a terrible time. Whatever your intentions, hand me that pistol. Then close your eyes, and count from one to a hundred, and I shall spare your life.

t_ch: 我不会询问你，你现在就可以闭眼数数了。
t_en: I won't ask twice. Close your eyes and start counting now.

t_ch: （照他说的做）
t_en: (Do as he says)

t_ch: *有什么掉进海里的声音*
t_en: *Something splashes into the ocean*

t_ch: <unbreak>98</unbreak>、<unbreak>99</unbreak>、<unbreak>100</unbreak>。
t_en: <unbreak>98</unbreak>, <unbreak>99</unbreak>, <unbreak>100</unbreak>.

t_ch: （假装照着做，数到<unbreak>20</unbreak>就睁眼）
t_en: (Pretend to do as he says and open your eyes on <unbreak>20</unbreak>)

t_ch: *有什么掉进海里的声音*
t_en: *Something splashes into the ocean*

t_ch: …<unbreak>18</unbreak>、<unbreak>19</unbreak>、<unbreak>20</unbreak>。
t_en: ...<unbreak>18</unbreak>, <unbreak>19</unbreak>, <unbreak>20</unbreak>.

t_ch: 海滩上空无一人。
t_en: There is no one to be seen on the beach.

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: （跃渊说护珠人已经认可了我的能力，我可以独立处理巡逻中产生的情况。）
t_en: (Yueyuan said that the Pearlkeepers have already recognized my talents and that I can deal with anything I encounter on patrol alone.)

t_ch: （<color=#dbc291ff>要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下</color>…今天还会有漂流瓶吗？）
t_en: (<color=#dbc291ff>Drive away The Unshackled and take care of the things they release</color>... I wonder if there will be any drift bottles today?)

t_ch: 这毫无疑问是一个漂流瓶，还是漂洋过海的那种。
t_en: This is undoubtedly a drift bottle, one that has crossed the ocean, too.

t_ch: （总之先打开看看，再考虑怎么办吧。）
t_en: (Let's take a look for now and decide what to do from there.)

t_ch: 落难者说自己已经过上了令人羡慕的生活，对此你应该如何看待呢？
t_en: The castaway says that they're living a life that would make others jealous, how are you to take this?

t_ch: 很不错，表达一下羡慕之情。
t_en: That's nice. Express your envy to them.

t_ch: （不知道漂流中的故事是真是假，但比起被人打扰、被人左右情绪的日子，我更喜欢无人问津的时光。）
t_en: (I don't know if this story from the waves is true, but I definitely prefer my moments of solitary peace to when I have others bothering and disrupting me.)

t_ch: 我会否定这种自以为是的说法。
t_en: I disagree with this self-righteous opinion.

t_ch: （不知道漂流中的故事是真是假，那种自我封闭、自我陶醉的逃避做法，不能代表所有人的想法不是吗？）
t_en: (I don't know if this story from the waves is true, but this insular, self-indulgent escapism could hardly be representative of everyone's ideals, don't you think?)

t_ch: 什么破漂流瓶，直接扔回海里！
t_en: Who cares about these dastardly drift bottles!? Just chuck it right back!

t_ch: （接下来的故事，不应该是帮助落难船长，制伏叛乱水手，重新回到文明世界吗？别擅自在荒岛上留下来了啊！）
t_en: (Their story from here should be about rescuing a castaway captain or subduing rebel sailors and returning to the civilized world, surely? You can't just selfishly hole up on a deserted island!)

t_ch: 在无法区分致敬和玩梗的世界里，它选择了半途而废，这是最不可以被原谅的。
t_en: In a world where no one can tell the difference between paying due respects to making everything a meme, they've chosen to stop halfway. This is unforgivable.

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: 嘿，你还记得吗？凡人在世间的种种行为与观念都是与命途相关的。有时一念之间，你就<color=#dbc291ff>产生了某个具体的倾向</color>。
t_en: Hey, do you remember? Every action and belief a mortal holds in this world is connected to their Path. Sometimes, a single thought <color=#dbc291ff>can cause you to stray in a certain direction</color>.

t_ch: 你对这种「避世隐居」的生活表达了羡慕之情，能进一步想想，到底是为什么吗？
t_en: You express a feeling of envy for this kind of "hermit lifestyle." Could you think a little further into why this is?

t_ch: 我很喜欢这种治愈心灵的感觉。
t_en: I really enjoy this feeling of spiritual healing.

t_ch: …这也许是「丰饶」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「丰饶」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「丰饶」开局如何？
t_en: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of Abundance. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of Abundance. How about trying Abundance at the start of your next Simulated Universe?

t_ch: 我只是想保留一些个人空间。
t_en: I just want to maintain some personal space.

t_ch: …这也许是「存护」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「存护」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「存护」开局如何？
t_en: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of Preservation. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of Preservation. How about trying out Preservation at the start of your next Simulated Universe run?

t_ch: 说真的，我就只是想摆烂而已。
t_en: Honestly, I just want to stop working.

t_ch: …这也许是「虚无」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「虚无」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「虚无」开局如何？
t_en: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of Nihility. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of Nihility. How about trying out Nihility at the start of your next Simulated Universe run?

t_ch: 我只是想试试不同选项的结果。
t_en: I just want to see what happens when I try something different.

t_ch: …这也许是「欢愉」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「欢愉」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「欢愉」开局如何？
t_en: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of Elation. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of Elation. How about trying out Elation at the start of your next Simulated Universe run?

t_ch: 也许接下来，你又走上了别的命途。谁也不知道……
t_en: Perhaps you shall set off along a different Path in the future. Who knows...

t_ch: （漂流瓶也不知道去哪了。看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (I have no idea where the drift bottle went. It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: 嘿，你还记得吗？凡人在世间的种种行为与观念都是与命途相关的。有时一念之间，你就<color=#dbc291ff>产生了某个具体的倾向</color>。
t_en: Hey, do you remember? Every action and belief a mortal holds in this world is connected to their Path. Sometimes, a single thought <color=#dbc291ff>can cause you to stray in a certain direction</color>.

t_ch: 你对这种「避世隐居」的生活表现出否定态度，能进一步想想，到底是为什么吗？
t_en: You expressed disapproval of this kind of "hermit lifestyle," could you think a little further into why this is?

t_ch: 因为这意味着和过去告别。
t_en: Because this means saying farewell to your past.

t_ch: …这也许是「记忆」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「记忆」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「记忆」开局如何？
t_en: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of Remembrance. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of Remembrance. How about trying out Remembrance at the start of your next Simulated Universe run?

t_ch: 因为我有着未竟之事要做。
t_en: Because I have things I still need to take care of.

t_ch: …这也许是「巡猎」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「巡猎」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「巡猎」开局如何？
t_en: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of The Hunt. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of The Hunt. How about trying out The Hunt at the start of your next Simulated Universe run?

t_ch: 这封信的态度让我有些生气！
t_en: Their attitude in that letter made me mad!

t_ch: …这也许是「毁灭」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「毁灭」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「毁灭」开局如何？
t_en: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of Destruction. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of Destruction. How about trying out Destruction at the start of your next Simulated Universe run?

t_ch: 我只是想试试不同选项的结果。
t_en: I just want to see what happens when I try something different.

t_ch: …这也许是「欢愉」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「欢愉」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「欢愉」开局如何？
t_en: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of Elation. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of Elation. How about trying out Elation at the start of your next Simulated Universe run?

t_ch: 也许接下来，你又走上了别的命途。谁也不知道……
t_en: Perhaps you shall set off along a different Path in the future. Who knows...

t_ch: （漂流瓶也不知道去哪了。看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (I have no idea where the drift bottle went. It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: （跃渊说护珠人已经认可了我的能力，我可以独立处理巡逻中产生的情况。）
t_en: (Yueyuan said that the Pearlkeepers have already recognized my talents and that I can deal with anything I encounter on patrol alone.)

t_ch: （<color=#dbc291ff>要是发现了放生帮就去威慑他们，要是发现了放生对象就处理一下</color>…今天还会有漂流瓶吗？）
t_en: (<color=#dbc291ff>Drive away The Unshackled and take care of the things they release</color>... I wonder if there will be any drift bottles today?)

t_ch: 这毫无疑问是一个漂流瓶，还是漂洋过海的那种。
t_en: This is undoubtedly a drift bottle, one that has crossed the ocean, too.

t_ch: （总之先打开看看，再考虑怎么办吧。）
t_en: (Let's take a look for now and decide what to do from there.)

t_ch: 这一次漂流瓶里装着灯神，你打算怎么做？
t_en: This time, there is a Genie stored inside the drift bottle. What will you do?

t_ch: 轻轻擦拭一下瓶身，表示尊敬。
t_en: Rub it gently to show respect.

t_ch: 主人，我是灯神，请问你有什么吩咐？
t_en: Master, I am the Genie, what are your orders?

t_ch: 什么破漂流瓶，直接扔回海里！
t_en: Who cares about these dastardly drift bottles!? Just chuck it right back!

t_ch: 愿望要是这么容易实现，那就不是愿望了。你将漂流瓶扔回了大海里。
t_en: If a wish were that easy to make true, it would not be called a wish. You threw the drift bottle back into the ocean.

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: 啊，我知道主人的疑惑。我可以先回答你的问题，放心，这不算在愿望里。
t_en: Ah, I understand your suspicion, master. Allow me to answer your questions first. Fear not, this will not count as your wish.

t_ch: 你在哪里？
t_en: Where are you?

t_ch: 我就在你的面前，如果你看不见，也许这说明我是*更高维度*的生物。
t_en: I am right in front of you. If you cannot see me, perhaps it is because I am a higher-dimensional existence.

t_ch: 没关系，我信守承诺，我会实现你的愿望。
t_en: Fear not, I honor my promises, I shall grant your wish.

t_ch: 为什么只有一个愿望？
t_en: Why do I only get one wish?

t_ch: 一个愿望有什么问题吗？您是对*数字*很介意吗？
t_en: Is there something wrong with granting just one wish? Do you have a problem with that number?

t_ch: 有些故事里是三个愿望，有些故事里是一个愿望，我这里只有一个。
t_en: Some stories have three wishes. Some have just one. I can only give one.

t_ch: 可千万别尝试「将『一个愿望』变成『三个愿望』」啊，这是我诚挚的警告，上一次这样尝试的人已经被关在瓶子里了。
t_en: But don't even think about making that one wish into three. I'll warn you now that the last person who tried that has already been sealed into a bottle.

t_ch: 来实现我的愿望吧！
t_en: Then, please grant my wish!

t_ch: 很好，你有什么愿望呢？
t_en: Very well. What is your wish?

t_ch: 不老的容颜。
t_en: To always look young.

t_ch: 你会有不老的容颜。
t_en: You shall forever look young.

t_ch: 花不完的钱。
t_en: Endless money.

t_ch: 只要你不断挣钱，你会有花不完的钱。
t_en: As long as you keep earning it, you will never run out of money.

t_ch: 美好的爱情。
t_en: A perfect romance.

t_ch: 你当然会有美好的爱情。
t_en: Of course you will have a perfect romance.

t_ch: 现在没想好，以后许愿。
t_en: I haven't decided yet. I'll make my wish later.

t_ch: 那么你以后还会遇到这个漂流瓶。
t_en: Then you shall encounter this drift bottle again.

t_ch: 你被永远关在瓶子里。
t_en: I wish for you to be shut inside this bottle forever.
You...

t_ch: 算你狠。
t_en: Fine. You win.

t_ch: 灯神似乎已经离开，愿望似乎也已经实现。
t_en: It seems that the Genie has left and your wish has been granted.

t_ch: （看来海滩也没有其他情况了，巡逻就到此结束吧。）
t_en: (It seems there is nothing else out of the ordinary happening along the beach. I'll end my patrol here.)

t_ch: 你找我是有什么事吗？
t_en: Did you want something?

t_ch: 我最近在研究「烈焰浓茶」的新口味，这店开了这么久，总归要让客人们有些新鲜感。
t_en: Lately, I've been working on new flavors for my Flaming Potent Tea. Innovation is the key to business, you know.

t_ch: 像我刚研发出来的「漆黑生酪」口味，听上去还蛮有噱头的，喝起来也相当不错，只是多种配料搅拌在一起才显得颜色奇怪，喝起来还是挺好喝的。
t_en: Check out this new Midnight Cream Tea flavor I just developed. It sounds pretty catchy and tastes great, but its color might look a little unusual with so many ingredients.

t_ch: 当然，像我这样自吹自擂可不行，还是得收集一些顾客的建议才好。
t_en: But of course, boasting about it like this does not a fine tea make. I need feedback and suggestions from my customers.

t_ch: 正巧你在，我这新品「漆黑生酪浓茶」的材料也齐全，你要不来两口尝尝？顺便给我提点意见？
t_en: Since you're here, why not try my new Midnight Cream Tea and let me know what you think?

t_ch: 免费的话…乐意至极！
t_en: I'd love to! ...If it's free.

t_ch: 哈哈，当然是免费的了，研发成本和前期测试的这点小钱都舍不得掏，我还做什么生意呀。
t_en: Of course it's free. If I can't cover the costs of research, development, and preliminary testing, what kind of business am I running?

t_ch: 你这茶有安全保障吗？
t_en: Can you guarantee my safety?

t_ch: 放心好了，配方的原材料都经过地衡司质检的，安心放心。而且我先前也喝过几次，你看我现在活蹦乱跳的，能有什么问题。
t_en: Don't worry. All my ingredients have passed the Realm-Keeping Commission's quality inspection. Plus, I've downed it a few times myself, and I'm still alive and kicking. What could possibly go wrong?

t_ch: 稍等，我这就去给您上茶！
t_en: Wait a few moments. Your tea will be ready in no time!

t_ch: 杜老板将调配好的「漆黑生酪浓茶」倒入茶杯中。
t_en: Boss Du pours the Midnight Cream Tea into your teacup.

t_ch: 来，客官，请——
t_en: Please enjoy!

t_ch: 一饮而尽！
t_en: Bottoms up!

t_ch: 你端起茶杯，揭开盖子，望着杯中漆黑一片又夹杂着某种可疑的蓝紫色液体，你犹豫了片刻。
t_en: You pick up the teacup, remove the lid, and gaze at the questionable blue-purple liquid in the cup. You can't help but hesitate for a moment.

t_ch: 「烈焰浓茶」新品的颜色让你想起了在星穹列车中凝望银河的时候，车窗外那片陌生又瑰丽的星空，静谧而壮丽，静谧得让你发自内心地恐惧。但话又说回来，星空不再静谧，这听起来反而更令人害怕……
t_en: The color of the new Flaming Potent Tea reminds you of the magnificent yet eerie starry sky outside the Astral Express. It is so quiet and magnificent that it inspires a sense of awe, even fear. But then again, if the starry sky were no longer quiet, it would be even more frightening...

t_ch: 杜老板面带笑意的目光将你从恐惧的深渊中拉了回来，但你分不清这究竟是往回拉，还是往前推。终于，你鼓起勇气，凝望着「烈焰浓茶」，直面着自己内心的恐惧，捏着鼻子将它一饮而尽！
t_en: Boss Du's smiling gaze snaps you back from the fearsome abyss, although you are not sure if he's pulling you away from it or pushing you towards it. In the end, you muster up your courage, stare directly at the Flaming Potent Tea and your inner fears, pinch your nose, and just like that, drink it up!

t_ch: 你仔细品味着，浓郁的酪香与鲜嫩多汁的水果混合在一起，清甜馥郁，除了果肉塞牙缝之外没有任何的问题——
t_en: You taste the tea carefully, savoring its rich creamy aroma and sweetness of the fresh fruity juice. There seems to be nothing wrong with this tea, except for the fruit pulp getting stuck between your teeth...

t_ch: 且慢，你忽然意识到它喝起来并不「燃」，没有辛辣的刺激感，这玩意真的还能算「烈焰浓茶」吗？
t_en: But then, you suddenly realize that it doesn't taste like it's "flaming" at all. What kind of Flaming Potent Tea would it be without that spicy kick?

t_ch: 怎么样？好喝吗？感觉如何？
t_en: How does it taste? Do you like it?

t_ch: 挺好喝的，就是不够燃。
t_en: It tastes good, but not "flaming" at all.

t_ch: 喝完喉咙没着火，算什么浓茶？
t_en: What kind of Flaming Potent Tea doesn't have a burning kick?

t_ch: 嗯…我当初也觉得挺好喝，色泽上看也很有噱头，反倒把「烈焰浓茶」的金字招牌抛之脑后了。
t_en: Yeah... I thought it tasted good and had a unique look, but now that you mention it, it really doesn't have anything to do with Flaming Potent Tea.

t_ch: 多亏你提醒了我…要是直接开卖了，那我们茶庄就沦为普普通通的饮品店了，特色全无，那怎么行！
t_en: Thank you for reminding me... otherwise, I'd have started selling it, and my tea house would have been reduced to an ordinary beverage store with no special features. That's simply unacceptable!

t_ch: 看来我的新品研发计划还得继续下去，可惜并不是每位顾客有时间……
t_en: Looks like I've got to develop more new flavors, but unfortunately, not all of my customers have the time to give me their feedback...

t_ch: 哦对，我怎么把你给忘了。正巧你在呢！怎么样，{NICKNAME}，来当我的「视茶员」如何？该有的报酬定不会短了你的。
t_en: Oh, how could I possibly forget you, {NICKNAME}! Why don't you try my new products and give me your thoughts? You'll receive compensation for your help, of course.

t_ch: 有报酬？包在我身上吧！
t_en: Compensation? Sure, leave it to me!

t_ch: 你放心，我们生意人最讲究信誉了，答应过你的我绝不会忘！
t_en: Don't worry! We business people are most reliable! I'll definitely make it worth your while!

t_ch: 我拿你当朋友你却羞辱我…
t_en: I treat you as a friend, but you treat me like a worker for hire...

t_ch: 哎呀，这倒是我的不是了。你说得对，相识一场，我们就不该谈这些庸俗的字眼。
t_en: Ah, my bad! I apologize sincerely for my misbehavior! You're right. We're good friends, and we shouldn't let material interests ruin that.

t_ch: 那这么着——我刚采购了一些有意思的配料，号称什么「闻起来臭吃起来香」，还打算添加到配方里呢，都是仙舟本地不多见的生鲜，你就当来尝尝鲜吧！
t_en: Here's the deal: I just got some interesting ingredients, and the peddler said "it smells terrible but tastes terrific." I'm planning to add it to my tea. These are all rare ingredients not usually found on Xianzhou. How about you help me taste test it?

t_ch: 今日这茶上不得台面，改日研发出新品了再喊你。
t_en: Well, this tea isn't up to par today, it seems. I'll give you a call once I've come up with something new.

t_ch: 你找我是有什么事吗？
t_en: Did you want something?

t_ch: 最近我偶然得到些灵感，先连以滋补药材和新鲜蔬果为配料，栽了好些跟头，好不容易才捣鼓出新的配方。
t_en: Lately, I've been trying to make something new with fresh herbs, vegetables, and fruits. After some setbacks, I've finally developed a new recipe.

t_ch: 这「烈焰浓茶」新品，已经按照你先前的建议，保证「燃」味十足。
t_en: This is the new flavor for Flaming Potent Tea. I've followed your suggestions from last time, and this one is sure to lift your spirits with its spicy kick.

t_ch: 来来来，且等我为你泡一杯试试。
t_en: Why don't you come over and give it a try?

t_ch: 杜老板为你亲自斟茶，倒了满满一杯茶。
t_en: Boss Du personally pours a cup of tea for you.

t_ch: 来来来，先喝这杯试试，你若是觉得这一杯还不够滋味，别急，喝了这一杯接着还有一坛子呢。
t_en: Here, feel free to give it a try, and if you feel like drinking more, there's plenty where that came from.

t_ch: 喝就完事了！
t_en: Bottoms up!
